Patch Set 5: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(5 inline comments)

The sanity-gss tests failed with:

 gss/krb5 is not supported
 sanity-gss : @@@@@@ FAIL: init_gss : GSS=true, but gss/krb5 is not supported! 

Should that script be passing at this point?  Marking -1 based on that result, but I can be convinced otherwise if the script is not expected to pass yet (i.e. needs some later patches to get it working), since we don't currently run it in any of our automated tests.

Patch Set 8:

Andy, I still see that the requested sanity-gss test (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sets/9c303dee-83bc-11e3-837d-52540035b04c) is still failing:

 gss/krb5 is not supported
 sanity-gss : @@@@@@ FAIL: init_gss : GSS=true, but gss/krb5 is not supported!

Sorry for the confusion in the Maloo results.  The "required" test runs review-dne and review-ldiskfs (which do not include sanity-gss) are passing and this results in Maloo reporting the patch verified.

Patch Set 8: Code-Review+1

Give +1 even though sanity-gss is failing in our test environment.  We do not currently run this test automatically, and Andy reports it is passing on our system, so this can land while our test/build environment is being debugged.

Patch Set 8: Code-Review+1

(2 comments)

I found that most of the codes in sanity-gss.sh and sanity-krb5.sh are common codes, like start_dbench(), check_dbench(), check_multiple_gss_daemons() and even most of the sub-test functions.

If the patch needs to be refreshed or there will be new patch, could you please make the common codes in one script? Please refer to the way used by parallel-scale-nfs.sh, parallel-scale-nfsv3.sh and parallel-scale-nfsv4.sh.
Patch Set 8: Code-Review+1

I should have made his other suggested changes, though, and will do so in a subsequent patch.

parents.subject: LU-2430 mdd: add lfs mv to migrate inode.

commit.subject: LU-4371 gss: Generalize testing of GSSAPI code
commit.message:
LU-4371 gss: Generalize testing of GSSAPI code

Rename sanity-gss.sh to sanity-krb5.sh and make sanity-gss.sh a
mechanism-agnostic test suite for just the GSSAPI code itself using
the gssnull security flavor, which in turn uses the null GSSAPI
mechanism.

Test-Parameters: testlist=sanity-gss
Signed-off-by: Andrew Korty <ajk@iu.edu>
Change-Id: I149ce66c4b044e20b9e8e1b93058ec4f62aba881
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/8525
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Jian Yu <jian.yu@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
